title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: **Objective** Implement a minimal two-step flow: `Belief -> philosophy-delta` (dry, auditable generation) and `philosophy-delta -> philosophy.md` (explicit manual promotion), without changing the runtime worldview source path used by skills. **Files to touch** - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/refresh-philosophy-delta.sh` - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/promote-philosophy-delta.sh` - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-philosophy-worldview-smoke.sh` **Acceptance criteria** - `refresh-philosophy-delta.sh` reads only Obsidian belief notes and writes a new file `/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/outputs/philosophy-delta.md` (or equivalent), never modifying `.claude/docs/philosophy.md` or `Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/philosophy.md` directly. - `promote-philosophy-delta.sh` copies/integrates only the generated delta into both - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/docs/philosophy.md` - `/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md` and does not touch skill prompts or runtime config files. - Existing runtime remains unchanged: all write/agent workflows continue to read from `~/.claude/docs/philosophy.md` only. - On malformed/empty belief extraction, refresh exits non-zero with no write to philosophy outputs, and smoke tests explicitly check this behavior. **Top 3 risks** - Belief schema drift in Obsidian (`frontmatter/sections`) can make extraction unstable. - Duplicate or conflicting belief statements can produce noisy or low-quality deltas if merging/sorting rules are weak. - Operational drift risk if users forget to promote; worldview stays stale unless manual promotion is documented and enforced in runbook.
